instructions: Listen to and read the information about how dogs became out best friend. Then share your thoughts.
advice: There are no right answers to the question. Just share your ideas.
title: How dogs became our best friend.

pages.text: Have you ever wondered why your dog looks at you like you're the most important person in the world? The answer goes back over 15,000 years.

Long ago, wild wolves lived near human camps. Most wolves stayed away, but some bolder ones crept closer, drawn by the smell of food and fire. The humans who fed these calmer wolves discovered something useful: the wolves would bark at danger and help on hunts. A friendship had begun.

Over thousands of years, these wolves changed. Because only the friendliest ones stayed close to humans, their cubs grew up gentler than wild wolves. Slowly, generation after generation, they became something new — dogs.

Different groups of people started breeding dogs for different jobs. Some dogs herded sheep. Others guarded homes or pulled sleds through snow. Each breed carried the memory of those ancient jobs in its body and its instincts.

Fau-chan is a Kai-Ken, a breed from the mountains of Japan. For centuries, his ancestors helped hunters track deer and wild boar through thick forests. When Fau-chan follows his nose on a walk, he is doing exactly what his great-great-grandparents did thousands of years ago.

questions.question: When your dog looks at you, what do you think he is thinking? Do you think dogs have feelings the same way humans do?
Imagine you are a child living 15,000 years olf. A wolf cub wanders into your camp. What would you do? Would you be scared or curious?
Dogs changed because of the choices humans made over thousands of years. Can you think of anything else in the world that changed slowly because of human choices?
The story says dogs became humans' best friends. But do you think humans also became dogs' best friends? What's the difference?
